declaration.code: class func `import`(_ domain: NSFileProviderDomain, fromDirectoryAt url: URL, completionHandler: @escaping @Sendable ((any Error)?) -> Void)

overview:
## Discussion

Use this method to migrate an existing file hierarchy on disk to a [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderExtension] without redownloading the data. After you call the method, the provided URL is no longer valid. The system has moved and now manages all of its contents.

If a domain with the same name already exists, the method fails with an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSFileWriteFileExistsError-swift.var] error. The URL remains untouched. If the system doesn’t allow the extension to request a migration, the method fails with an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSFeatureUnsupportedError-swift.var] error.

The system starts by moving the provided directory into its local cache, and then calls the completion handler. Then, for each item in the directory, it calls your extension’s [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderReplicatedExtension/createItem(basedOn:fields:contents:options:request:completionHandler:)] with the [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderCreateItemOptions/mayAlreadyExist] option.

When the import finishes, the system calls your extension’s [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderReplicatedExtension/importDidFinish(completionHandler:)] method. If you call [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderManager/reimportItems(below:completionHandler:)] before the import finishes, the system makes a single call to [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderReplicatedExtension/importDidFinish(completionHandler:)] for both imports.

# import(_:fromDirectoryAt:completionHandler:)

**Type Method**

Creates a new domain that takes ownership of on-disk data that your app previously managed without a file provider.

## Declaration

```swift
class func `import`(_ domain: NSFileProviderDomain, fromDirectoryAt url: URL, completionHandler: @escaping @Sendable ((any Error)?) -> Void)
```

```swift
class func `import`(_ domain: NSFileProviderDomain, fromDirectoryAt url: URL) async throws
```

## Parameters

- **domain**: The domain to import.
- **url**: A URL that points to the directory to import.
- **completionHandler**: A block that the system calls as soon as it creates the new domain. It takes the following parameters:



## Discussion



Use this method to migrate an existing file hierarchy on disk to a [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderExtension] without redownloading the data. After you call the method, the provided URL is no longer valid. The system has moved and now manages all of its contents.

If a domain with the same name already exists, the method fails with an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSFileWriteFileExistsError-swift.var] error. The URL remains untouched. If the system doesn’t allow the extension to request a migration, the method fails with an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSFeatureUnsupportedError-swift.var] error.

The system starts by moving the provided directory into its local cache, and then calls the completion handler. Then, for each item in the directory, it calls your extension’s [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderReplicatedExtension/createItem(basedOn:fields:contents:options:request:completionHandler:)] with the [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderCreateItemOptions/mayAlreadyExist] option.

When the import finishes, the system calls your extension’s [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderReplicatedExtension/importDidFinish(completionHandler:)] method. If you call [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderManager/reimportItems(below:completionHandler:)] before the import finishes, the system makes a single call to [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderReplicatedExtension/importDidFinish(completionHandler:)] for both imports.

## Working with domains

- **init(for:)**: Returns a newly created file provider manager for the specified domain.
- **add(_:completionHandler:)**: Adds a domain to the File Provider extension.
- **getDomainsWithCompletionHandler(_:)**: Returns all of the File Provider extension’s domains.
- **remove(_:completionHandler:)**: Removes a domain from the File Provider extension.
- **remove(_:mode:completionHandler:)**: Removes a domain from the File Provider extension using the specified options.
- **removeAllDomains(completionHandler:)**: Removes all domains from the File Provider extension.
- **NSFileProviderManager.DomainRemovalMode**: A mode indicating how the system handles user data when removing a domain.
- **disconnect(reason:options:completionHandler:)**: Disconnects the domain from the extension.
- **NSFileProviderManager.DisconnectionOptions**: Options for disconnecting a domain from the extension.
- **reconnect(completionHandler:)**: Reconnects the domain with the extension.
- **waitForStabilization(completionHandler:)**: Requests a notification after the domain stabilizes.
- **temporaryDirectoryURL()**: Returns the URL of a directory that the File Provider extension can use to temporarily store files before passing them to the system.
